[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, given a maker-signed Merkle root that commits to an offer with a specific tick, execute that offer at a later block.timestamp where timeToMaturity = zeroFloorSub(offer.market.maturity, block.timestamp) is significantly smaller, causing settlementFee(id, timeToMaturity) to be higher than when the maker signed, so that sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee is lower than the maker intended, systematically leaking value from the maker to the protocol fee pool? Preconditions: maker signs root at TTM=360 days; taker waits until TTM=1 day; offer.expiry is far in the future. Call sequence: EcrecoverRatifier.isRatified (passes, root still valid) -> Midnight.take (computes higher settlementFee, lower sellerPrice). Invariant tested: ratifier-approved offer must not allow the maker to receive less than the price they committed to at signing time. Scoped impact: systematic value leakage from maker to claimableSettlementFee. Proof idea: fuzz (tick, TTM_sign, TTM_execute, maxUnits) and assert sellerAssets_at_execute < sellerAssets_at_sign for same units.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that HashLib.hashOffer commits to offer.maxAssets but not to the settlement fee, so that a maker who signed maxAssets=X intending to cap total buyer-side cost at X finds that at a different TTM the same maxAssets allows more or fewer units to be filled (because buyerAssets per unit changes with fee), causing the offer to be economically over- or under-filled relative to maker intent? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets=X, maker signs at TTM=180 days; taker executes at TTM=1 day where fee is lower. Call sequence: isRatified (passes) -> take -> consumed[maker][group] += buyerAssets (mulDivDown). Invariant tested: consumed tracking must reflect maker's economic intent encoded in maxAssets. Scoped impact: extractable value via TTM-shifted fee reducing per-unit buyerAssets, allowing more units than maker intended. Proof idea: unit test with fixed maxAssets, vary TTM, assert total units filled exceeds maker's intended capacity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, for a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with maxAssets set to cap sellerAssets, exploit mulDivUp rounding in sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD) to cause consumed[maker][group] to increment by a value that rounds up, so that the offer exhausts faster than the maker intended, effectively reducing the maker's total filled units below maxAssets/sellerPrice? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, offer.maxAssets>0, sellerPrice not a divisor of WAD. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> consumed += sellerAssets (mulDivUp). Invariant tested: offer fill capacity must not be systematically reduced by rounding direction. Scoped impact: systematic value leakage via premature offer exhaustion. Proof idea: fuzz (units, tick, settlementFee) and assert sum(sellerAssets_per_fill) > maxAssets after fewer fills than expected.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, for a buy offer (offer.buy=true) with maxAssets set, exploit mulDivDown rounding in buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) so that consumed[maker][group] increments by a value that rounds down, allowing the taker to fill slightly more total units than maxAssets/buyerPrice, extracting value beyond the maker's intended cap? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, offer.maxAssets>0, buyerPrice not a divisor of WAD, many small partial fills. Call sequence: isRatified (passes each time) -> take (consumed += mulDivDown(buyerAssets)) repeated. Invariant tested: total units filled must not exceed maxAssets / buyerPrice due to rounding. Scoped impact: extractable value via rounding-induced overconsumption of maker's offer. Proof idea: fuzz (units_per_fill, tick, fee) and assert sum(units_filled) * buyerPrice > maxAssets * WAD.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the piecewise linear interpolation in settlementFee(id, timeToMaturity) = (feeLower*(end-TTM) + feeUpper*(TTM-start)) / (end-start) where integer division truncates, so that at specific TTM values the fee rounds down by up to (end-start-1) wei, causing buyerAssets - sellerAssets < actual fee owed, leaking protocol fee revenue? Preconditions: market with non-zero settlement fee cbps, TTM near a breakpoint boundary. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets. Invariant tested: claimableSettlementFee must equal the sum of all settlement fees charged. Scoped impact: systematic protocol fee undercollection. Proof idea: fuzz TTM across [0, 360 days] and assert settlementFee(id, TTM) * units == buyerAssets - sellerAssets for all unit amounts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, by choosing units=1 wei, cause buyerAssets = mulDivDown(1, buyerPrice, WAD) = 0 (since buyerPrice < WAD), so that the taker receives 1 unit of credit while paying 0 loan tokens, extracting free credit from the maker? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, tick > 0 (price < 1 WAD), units=1. Call sequence: isRatified (passes) -> take -> buyerAssets=0, sellerAssets=0, consumed += 0 (if maxAssets) or 1 (if maxUnits). Invariant tested: every credit increase must correspond to a valid asset transfer. Scoped impact: direct loss - free credit extraction. Proof idea: unit test with units=1, high tick (low price), assert buyerAssets > 0 or take reverts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that buyerPendingFeeIncrease = mulDivDown(buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) rounds down, so that at TTM near 0 (post-maturity take where timeToMaturity=0) the pending fee is exactly 0, allowing a lender to acquire credit with zero continuous fee obligation, systematically leaking continuous fee revenue? Preconditions: market post-maturity, offer.buy=true, continuousFee>0. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> buyerPendingFeeIncrease = mulDivDown(units * continuousFee * 0, WAD) = 0. Invariant tested: continuous fee must accrue proportionally to credit and TTM. Scoped impact: systematic continuous fee leakage for post-maturity credit acquisitions. Proof idea: unit test at block.timestamp = maturity + 1, assert pendingFee increase is 0 and quantify revenue loss.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing a maker's signed sell offer (offer.buy=false) at TTM just above a fee breakpoint boundary (e.g., TTM = 7 days + 1 second), cause the interpolated settlementFee to be slightly higher than at TTM = 7 days, so that sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee is slightly lower, and the maker (seller) receives fewer sellerAssets than if executed 1 second earlier, creating a time-sensitive value extraction window? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, TTM near breakpoint, maker is seller. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD). Invariant tested: maker's received assets must not be manipulable by taker's choice of execution timestamp. Scoped impact: extractable value via timestamp manipulation near fee breakpoints. Proof idea: differential test comparing sellerAssets at TTM=7d vs TTM=7d+1s for same units.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.hashOffer] Can an unprivileged taker construct two different ratifierData payloads (different proof paths in the Merkle tree) that both verify against the same root and the same offer hash, allowing the same signed root to authorize the same offer via two different proof paths, and if consumed tracking is per-group, does this create any double-spend or accounting confusion? Preconditions: maker signs a root; two valid Merkle proofs exist for the same leaf. Call sequence: isRatified(offer, ratifierData1) -> take; isRatified(offer, ratifierData2) -> take. Invariant tested: a single offer authorization must not be usable via multiple proof paths to bypass consumption limits. Scoped impact: offer replay via alternative proof paths. Proof idea: construct a Merkle tree with duplicate leaves and verify both proofs pass isLeaf.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.offerTreeTypeHash] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that offerTreeTypeHash(proof.length) uses proof.length as the tree height, so that the same offer leaf can be placed at different heights in different trees (height 1 vs height 5), and if a maker signs two roots of different heights containing the same offer, the consumed[maker][group] is shared, allowing the taker to fill the offer from either root until the group budget is exhausted, potentially at different fee/TTM conditions? Preconditions: maker signs root_h1 (height 1) and root_h5 (height 5) both containing the same offer. Call sequence: isRatified with root_h1 -> take; isRatified with root_h5 -> take. Invariant tested: offer consumption must be bounded by maxUnits/maxAssets regardless of which root is used. Scoped impact: offer overfill via multi-root consumption. Proof idea: unit test with two roots of different heights, same offer, assert consumed never exceeds maxUnits.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the rounding in tickToPrice where the price is rounded to PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP=1e12 multiples via divHalfDownUnchecked, so that for adjacent ticks the price difference is exactly 1e12, and the resulting buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) has a rounding error of up to (WAD-1)/WAD per unit, causing systematic underpayment by the buyer across many fills? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, tick chosen so buyerPrice is near a PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP boundary. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD). Invariant tested: buyer must pay at least units * buyerPrice / WAD rounded correctly. Scoped impact: systematic value leakage from maker to buyer via price rounding. Proof idea: fuzz tick values and assert buyerAssets * WAD >= units * buyerPrice - units for all units.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, for a buy offer with maxUnits-based consumption, exploit the fact that consumed[maker][group] += units (exact, no rounding) while buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) rounds down, so that the taker pays slightly less than units * buyerPrice / WAD per fill, and across N fills the total underpayment is up to N * (WAD-1) / WAD wei, extracting value from the maker? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, offer.maxUnits>0, buyerPrice not divisor of WAD, many fills. Call sequence: isRatified -> take (N times) -> consumed += units each time. Invariant tested: total buyerAssets paid must equal sum(units_i * buyerPrice / WAD) rounded correctly. Scoped impact: extractable value via mulDivDown rounding on buyerAssets across many fills. Proof idea: fuzz N fills with units=1, assert total_buyerAssets >= N * mulDivDown(1, buyerPrice, WAD).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the asymmetry between buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) and sellerAssets = mulDivDown(units, sellerPrice, WAD) for buy offers, where both round down, so that buyerAssets - sellerAssets (the settlement fee collected) could be 0 even when settlementFee > 0, if units is small enough that units * settlementFee < WAD, causing the protocol to collect zero fee for a valid trade? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, units small (e.g., 1), settlementFee > 0 but units * settlementFee < WAD. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> claimableSettlementFee += buyerAssets - sellerAssets = 0. Invariant tested: protocol must collect non-zero settlement fee whenever settlementFee > 0 and units > 0. Scoped impact: systematic protocol fee leakage for small-unit trades. Proof idea: unit test with units=1, settlementFee=1e12 (1 CBP), assert buyerAssets > sellerAssets.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, for a sell offer (offer.buy=false), exploit the fact that buyerAssets = mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD) and sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD) both round up, so that the taker (buyer) pays more than expected per unit, but the maker (seller) also receives more than expected, and the fee collected (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) could be 0 or negative if both round up by the same amount, creating a fee accounting inconsistency? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, units chosen so both mulDivUp computations round up by 1. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> claimableSettlementFee += buyerAssets - sellerAssets. Invariant tested: buyerAssets - sellerAssets must equal the settlement fee for the given units. Scoped impact: fee accounting corruption. Proof idea: differential test: assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets == units * settlementFee / WAD (rounded) for all (units, tick, TTM).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the EcrecoverRatifier domain separator uses block.chainid (live chain ID) while Midnight.toId uses INITIAL_CHAIN_ID (captured at construction), so that after a hard fork where block.chainid changes, previously signed roots become invalid in the ratifier but the market IDs remain valid, causing all maker offers using EcrecoverRatifier to be permanently frozen (unable to be filled), while the market continues to operate? Preconditions: hard fork changes block.chainid; maker has signed roots under old chainid. Call sequence: isRatified -> keccak256(EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH, block.chainid, address(this)) produces new separator -> ecrecover returns wrong signer -> Unauthorized(). Invariant tested: offer authorization must remain consistent with market state after chain events. Scoped impact: fund freeze - all EcrecoverRatifier-authorized offers become unfillable post-fork. Proof idea: unit test simulating chainid change, assert isRatified reverts for previously valid signatures.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, by choosing units such that units * buyerPrice overflows uint256 in mulDivDown/mulDivUp (since UtilsLib.mulDivDown uses unchecked multiplication x*y), cause an arithmetic overflow that produces a small buyerAssets value, allowing the taker to acquire a large number of units while paying almost nothing? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, units near type(uint256).max / buyerPrice + 1. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> buyerAssets = (units * buyerPrice) / WAD overflows. Invariant tested: buyerAssets must be monotonically increasing in units. Scoped impact: direct loss - free credit extraction via overflow. Proof idea: fuzz units near overflow boundary and assert buyerAssets >= units * buyerPrice / WAD without overflow.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that sellerPendingFeeDecrease = mulDivUp(sellerPos.pendingFee * sellerCreditDecrease, sellerPos.credit) rounds up, so that when a seller partially reduces their credit via a buy offer take, their pendingFee is reduced by slightly more than proportional, causing the seller to owe less continuous fee than they should, leaking continuous fee revenue? Preconditions: seller has existing credit and pendingFee; offer.buy=true; partial fill reduces sellerCreditDecrease < sellerPos.credit. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> sellerPendingFeeDecrease = mulDivUp(pendingFee * creditDecrease, credit). Invariant tested: pendingFee must decrease proportionally to credit decrease. Scoped impact: systematic continuous fee leakage via rounding in pendingFee accounting. Proof idea: fuzz (credit, pendingFee, creditDecrease) and assert sellerPendingFeeDecrease <= pendingFee * creditDecrease / credit + 1.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: cancelRoot / isRatified] Can an unprivileged authorized operator (authorized via IMidnight.isAuthorized) cancel all of a maker's Merkle roots by calling cancelRoot(maker, root) for each root, permanently preventing the maker's signed offers from being filled, causing a fund freeze where the maker cannot lend/borrow via their pre-signed offer trees? Preconditions: attacker is authorized by maker (e.g., via a compromised or malicious operator); maker has active roots. Call sequence: setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, maker) -> cancelRoot(maker, root1), cancelRoot(maker, root2), ... Invariant tested: root cancellation must only be callable by the maker or explicitly authorized parties. Scoped impact: fund freeze - maker's offer trees permanently invalidated. Proof idea: unit test where authorized operator cancels all roots and verifies isRatified reverts with RootCanceled.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / HashLib.isLeaf] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the leafIndex boundary check (leafIndex >> proof.length == 0) by passing proof.length=0 and leafIndex=0, so that the Merkle proof degenerates to root == hashOffer(offer), meaning any offer whose hash equals a root that the maker has signed (even if the maker signed it as a root of a larger tree) can be ratified with an empty proof, bypassing the tree structure? Preconditions: maker has signed a root that happens to equal the hash of some offer (e.g., a height-0 tree). Call sequence: isRatified(offer, abi.encode(sig, hashOffer(offer), 0, [])) -> isLeaf(root=hashOffer(offer), leafHash=hashOffer(offer), 0, []) returns true. Invariant tested: Merkle proof verification must not degenerate to trivial equality checks. Scoped impact: gate bypass - unauthorized offer ratification. Proof idea: unit test with empty proof and root = hashOffer(offer), verify isRatified passes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the offer hash commits to offer.market.maturity but not to the settlement fee schedule (settlementFeeCbp0..6), so that if the fee setter changes the market's settlement fee between when the maker signed the offer and when the taker executes it, the maker's offer is filled at a different spread than intended, causing value leakage to or from the maker? Preconditions: maker signs offer; feeSetter calls setMarketSettlementFee to change cbps; taker executes. Call sequence: isRatified (passes, fee change not in hash) -> take (uses new fee). Invariant tested: maker's signed offer must bind to the fee conditions at signing time. Scoped impact: extractable value via fee schedule change between signing and execution. Proof idea: unit test: sign offer, change fee, execute, assert sellerAssets differs from maker's expectation.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the wExp Taylor series approximation in tickToPrice (using only 3 terms: 1 + r + r^2/2 + r^3/6) to find ticks where the approximation error causes tickToPrice to return a price that is slightly higher or lower than the true exponential, and then use this price discrepancy to extract value from the maker by choosing ticks where the approximation favors the taker? Preconditions: tick chosen near the boundary of the approximation's accuracy. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> offerPrice = tickToPrice(tick) -> buyerAssets/sellerAssets computed from approximated price. Invariant tested: tickToPrice must be monotonically non-increasing and accurate to within PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP. Scoped impact: extractable value via price approximation error. Proof idea: differential test comparing tickToPrice(tick) against a high-precision exp implementation for all valid ticks.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, for a sell offer (offer.buy=false) with maxAssets-based consumption, exploit the fact that consumed += sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD) rounds up, while the actual sellerAssets transferred to the seller also rounds up, so that the offer exhausts (consumed >= maxAssets) after fewer units than maxAssets / sellerPrice, causing the maker to lend fewer units than intended and lose potential yield? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, offer.maxAssets>0, sellerPrice not divisor of WAD. Call sequence: isRatified -> take (multiple times) -> consumed += mulDivUp(sellerAssets). Invariant tested: total units filled must approach maxAssets / sellerPrice from below. Scoped impact: systematic value leakage - maker lends fewer units than intended. Proof idea: fuzz (units_per_fill, sellerPrice) and assert total_units_filled >= floor(maxAssets / sellerPrice) - 1.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the interaction between the settlement fee's piecewise linear interpolation and the PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP=1e12 in tickToPrice, where the fee is computed in WAD precision but the price is rounded to 1e12 multiples, causing the effective spread (buyerPrice - sellerPrice) to differ from the intended settlement fee by up to 1e12 wei per unit, creating systematic fee accounting errors? Preconditions: market with non-zero settlement fee; tick chosen so offerPrice is near a PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP boundary. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee (no rounding) -> buyerPrice = sellerPrice + settlementFee -> buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD). Invariant tested: buyerAssets - sellerAssets must equal units * settlementFee / WAD (rounded). Scoped impact: fee accounting corruption. Proof idea: assert (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) * WAD == units * settlementFee for all (units, tick, TTM).

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker, by executing a maker's signed offer at exactly block.timestamp == offer.market.maturity (TTM=0), cause timeToMaturity = zeroFloorSub(maturity, block.timestamp) = 0, so that buyerPendingFeeIncrease = mulDivDown(buyerCreditIncrease * continuousFee * 0, WAD) = 0, and also settlementFee uses the cbp0 breakpoint (0-day fee), potentially allowing the taker to acquire credit at the lowest possible fee with zero continuous fee obligation? Preconditions: offer.buy=true, block.timestamp == maturity, continuousFee > 0. Call sequence: isRatified -> take at maturity boundary -> pendingFee = 0. Invariant tested: continuous fee must be charged for credit acquired at maturity. Scoped impact: systematic continuous fee leakage at maturity boundary. Proof idea: unit test at block.timestamp = maturity, assert pendingFee increase is 0 and quantify.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isRatified / take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that the offer hash includes offer.tick but the settlement fee is computed from TTM at execution time, so that a maker who signed a sell offer at tick T expecting to receive sellerPrice = tickToPrice(T) - fee_at_signing actually receives sellerPrice = tickToPrice(T) - fee_at_execution, and if fee_at_execution > fee_at_signing (TTM decreased), the maker receives less than tickToPrice(T) - fee_at_signing, creating extractable value for the protocol at the maker's expense? Preconditions: offer.buy=false, TTM decreases between signing and execution. Call sequence: isRatified -> take -> sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee(id, TTM_execution). Invariant tested: maker's net received assets must match their signed price commitment. Scoped impact: extractable value from maker to protocol fee pool. Proof idea: fuzz (TTM_sign, TTM_execute) and assert sellerAssets_execute >= sellerAssets_sign for same units.
